title: Add author card + medically reviewed badge to condition/treatment pages
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Condition and treatment pages are anonymous clinical content — no visible author or reviewer. Add the existing author-card.html include and a 'Medically reviewed by Dr. Ryan Stewart, DO' badge to all /conditions/ and /treatments/ pages. Anonymous medical content is a top YMYL penalty risk.
close_reason: Added author-card with medically reviewed badge to page.html layout; switched 25 treatment pages to layout:page; added last_modified_date to all condition/treatment pages

title: Add Doctor/MedicalBusiness/LocalBusiness structured data schema
description: The site only has basic WebSite schema auto-generated by jekyll-seo-tag. For a medical practice site, this misses the most valuable schema types that can trigger rich results and knowledge panels. Add JSON-LD structured data for: (1) Physician schema — name, credentials, specialty, medical school, fellowship, (2) MedicalBusiness schema — practice address, phone, hours, insurance, (3) MedicalWebPage schema on condition/treatment pages. Implement via a custom _includes/schema.html partial included in the default layout.

title: Add Key Takeaways summary boxes to condition pages
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Add summary/key takeaways boxes at top of long condition guides. Improves featured snippet potential and readability. Use existing callout styling.

title: Add prominent scheduling CTA with phone number
description: From healthcare SEO audit: 'Schedule an Appointment' section has no actionable phone number or booking link. Add visible phone number and/or online scheduling link in header/footer. Add click-to-call on mobile.

title: Remove 'Just the Docs' footer link
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Footer displays 'This site uses Just the Docs, a documentation theme for Jekyll' with a link. Looks like a template site and undermines professional credibility for a medical practice.

title: Programmatic SEO: Location, Question, Comparison, and Persona Pages
description: Programmatic SEO strategy to attract urinary incontinence and prolapse patients from NE Wisconsin and UP Michigan. Goal: maximize Dr. Stewart's name/face visibility. See PSEO-PLAN.md for full details. ~206 pages across 6 phases.

title: Phase 1: Location pages for top 10 cities (~40 pages)
description: Create location pages for top 10 cities (Green Bay, Appleton, Oshkosh, Fond du Lac, Manitowoc, Sheboygan, Marquette, Escanaba, Menominee, Iron Mountain) x 4 conditions each. Includes: _data/locations.yml, _layouts/location.html, hub page at /locations/, Physician+MedicalWebPage schema. Each page has drive time, local context, state-specific insurance, telehealth callout, doctor photo+credentials, condition content, CTA.

title: Phase 2: Long-tail question pages for incontinence + OAB (~30 pages)
description: Expand existing uterine prolapse Q&A pattern to urinary incontinence (~15-20 questions) and overactive bladder (~10 questions). Each page: author card with photo, expert answer 200-400 words, FAQ schema via existing include, link to parent condition, CTA.

title: Phase 3: Treatment comparison pages (~8 pages)
description: Create treatment vs treatment comparison pages: Pessary vs Surgery, Sling vs Bulking Agents, Botox vs Medication for OAB, Native Tissue vs Sacrocolpopexy, Robotic vs Vaginal Repair, Mesh vs Non-Mesh Sling, SNM vs Botox for OAB, PT vs Surgery for Prolapse. Each: side-by-side table, pros/cons, Dr. Stewart perspective, candidate info, FAQ schema.

title: Phase 4: Location pages for remaining cities (~96 pages)
description: Expand location pages to remaining NE WI cities (Sturgeon Bay, Marinette, Oconto, Kewaunee, Two Rivers, De Pere, Neenah, Menasha, Kaukauna, Clintonville, Waupaca, New London, Brillion, Chilton) and UP MI cities (Sault Ste. Marie, Houghton, Hancock, Manistique, Munising, Gladstone, Negaunee, Ishpeming, Iron River, Crystal Falls). Same template as Phase 1.

title: Phase 5: Persona pages (~12 pages)
description: Create persona/life-stage pages: Prolapse After Hysterectomy, Incontinence After Childbirth, Incontinence During Menopause, POP in Younger Women, Prolapse After Pregnancy, Incontinence in Athletes, Recurrent Prolapse, Bladder Problems After C-Section, Prolapse and Sexual Function, Incontinence After Weight Gain, Prolapse in Women Who Lift, Bladder Leakage During Exercise.

title: Phase 6: Remaining question pages (~20 pages)
description: Expand Q&A to cystocele/rectocele questions (~7) and fecal incontinence questions (~4), plus remaining long-tail queries. Same pattern as Phase 2.

title: Cross-cutting: Omnipresence infrastructure (author card, physician schema, title pattern)
description: Build shared components used across all pSEO pages: 1) Author card include with photo/name/credentials, 2) Physician schema include, 3) MedicalWebPage schema include, 4) Consistent title suffix pattern, 5) Testimonial snippet include. Publish testimonials page. These are prerequisites for all phases.

title: Create privacy policy page with HIPAA language
description: From healthcare SEO audit: No privacy policy page exists. Required for any healthcare site. Should include HIPAA notice language, data collection practices, and patient privacy commitments. Major trust signal gap for YMYL compliance.
close_reason: Created privacy-policy.md with HIPAA language, added footer link

title: Quarterly clinical content review and citation updates
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Establish quarterly review cadence — verify clinical accuracy, update review dates, refresh citations as new guidelines are published. Healthcare content staleness is a ranking risk and patient safety issue.

title: Add default Open Graph image for social sharing
description: No og:image tags detected on any page. When the site is shared on social media, messaging apps, or AI tools, there is no preview image — reducing engagement and click-through. Fix: create a branded OG image (1200x630px) at /assets/images/og-default.jpg, then add a defaults block in _config.yml to set image: /assets/images/og-default.jpg for all pages. jekyll-seo-tag will automatically generate the og:image and twitter:image meta tags from this.

title: Change homepage H1 from generic welcome to keyword-rich heading
description: The homepage title tag is excellent (Urogynecologist in Green Bay, WI | Ryan Stewart, DO) but the H1 is 'Welcome. I'm glad you're here.' — containing zero keywords. The H1 is the strongest on-page ranking signal and should align with the target keyword. Fix: change the H1 in index.md to 'Urogynecologist in Green Bay, WI' and keep the welcoming tone in the subtitle paragraph below. File: index.md line 8.

title: Add citations to clinical content pages
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Zero citations on clinical pages. Stats like '80-90% cure rate' (sling page) and 'one in three women' (location pages) have no sources. Add References sections linking to PubMed, AUA/AUGS/ACOG guidelines, and Dr. Stewart's own publications where relevant. Start with the 10 highest-traffic pages.
close_reason: Added References sections with PubMed/guideline citations to 10 key clinical pages

title: Create accessibility statement
description: From healthcare SEO audit: No ADA/WCAG accessibility statement on the site. Create an accessibility page stating compliance commitment (WCAG 2.1 AA target).

title: Add NPI to Physician schema and author bio
description: From healthcare SEO audit: NPI 1922366061 is verified active but not displayed anywhere on the site. Add as 'identifier' property in Physician schema and display in author bio. Verifiable credential signal for quality raters.

title: Fix blog post permalink structure to remove category spaces from URLs
description: Blog post URLs include category names with spaces, producing encoded URLs like /pelvic%20health/urogynecology/women's%20health/2024/10/14/reclaiming-joyful-moments... The %20-encoded spaces look spammy in SERPs and reduce CTR. Fix: add permalink: /blog/:title/ to _config.yml, then add redirect_from entries on each existing post pointing to their old category-based URLs to preserve any existing link equity. This affects 7 published blog posts.

title: Add FAQ schema to treatment pages
description: All 12 condition pages have FAQ schema (via faq frontmatter + faq-schema-content.html include) generating rich snippets in SERPs, but none of the ~24 treatment pages do. Adding FAQ frontmatter data to treatment pages would create additional rich snippet opportunities. Priority treatment pages: sling procedures, pessary, botox injections, robotic sacrocolpopexy, pelvic floor physical therapy. Each needs 3-6 common patient questions with concise answers added to the YAML frontmatter faq array.

title: Add visible 'Last medically reviewed' date to clinical pages
description: From healthcare SEO audit: lastReviewed exists in MedicalWebPage schema but is not visible to users or quality raters. Add a visible 'Last medically reviewed: [date]' near the top of all condition and treatment pages. Distinguish from 'last modified' in footer.

title: Fix heading hierarchy on homepage — H3s should be H2s
description: The homepage index.md uses ### (H3) for section headings (My Expertise, About Me, My Approach, Schedule an Appointment) with no parent H2, skipping a heading level. This violates semantic HTML hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3) and is a minor accessibility/SEO issue. Fix: change all ### to ## in index.md. The Just the Docs theme styling can be preserved with CSS classes if needed.

title: Ensure Google Scholar profile links to ryanstewart.com
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Verify Google Scholar profile has ryanstewart.com as the homepage URL. Creates a two-way authority signal connecting published research to the website.

title: Add 'When to seek help' sections to condition pages
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Condition pages lack 'when to see a doctor' sections. Add these as patient safety signals and potential featured snippet content. Important for YMYL compliance.

title: Fix _config.yml url to include https:// protocol
description: The url field in _config.yml is set to ryanstewart.com without the https:// prefix. This causes jekyll-seo-tag to generate malformed canonical URLs and Open Graph tags, and makes the local build produce http://localhost:4000 URLs in the sitemap and robots.txt. Fix: change to url: https://ryanstewart.com. Highest-priority SEO fix — affects every page's canonical URL, OG metadata, sitemap entries, and robots.txt sitemap reference.

title: Add Google Scholar sameAs to Physician schema
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Add sameAs property to Physician schema linking to Google Scholar profile (https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=e8TJzPIAAAAJ). Connects structured data to research authority. Also link from research page and author bio.

title: Update footer copyright year from 2025 to 2026
description: The footer_content in _config.yml shows 'Copyright 2016-2025' but the current year is 2026. Update to 2016-2026. Minor trust signal — an outdated copyright year can make a site look unmaintained. One-line change in _config.yml footer_content field.

title: pSEO for long-tail insurance keywords (location + insurance plan)
description: Create programmatic SEO pages targeting searches like 'urogynecologist that accepts Aetna near Green Bay' or 'pelvic floor doctor Medicaid Appleton WI'. Full WI insurance list saved in Claude memory (project_insurance_list.md). MI insurance list TBD — Ryan is compiling it. Could pair location × insurance plan for high-value long-tail pages.

title: Update research page with all Google Scholar publications
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Research page lists ~20 publications but Google Scholar shows 44. Add missing entries including 2025-2026 publications (barbed suture RCT, bupivacaine RCT, fitness trackers study, QR code education QI project).

title: Increase blog publishing cadence to 1-2 posts/month
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Last blog post was May 2025 (~10 months ago). Regular clinical education posts build topical authority. Aim for 1-2 per month mixing clinical topics with patient education.

title: Link own publications on relevant treatment/condition pages
description: From healthcare SEO audit: Dr. Stewart's sling book chapter should be referenced on the sling procedures page. Prolapse surgery research on prolapse pages. Pessary research on pessary page. Readability research on the editorial standards page. 44 publications available — cross-reference where relevant.

title: Create editorial standards page
description: From healthcare SEO audit: No editorial standards/policy page. Create a page explaining that all content is written and reviewed by a fellowship-trained urogynecologist with 44 peer-reviewed publications. Strong E-E-A-T signal that competitors lack.

title: Publish unpublished long-tail uterine prolapse Q&A pages
description: 7+ pages targeting high-value patient search queries are set to published: false and not being indexed. These include: can-uterine-prolapse-be-reversed, how-does-uterine-prolapse-feel, how-fast-does-uterine-prolapse-progress, what-causes-uterine-prolapse, can-uterine-prolapse-cause-bleeding, can-uterine-prolapse-cause-cramping, can-your-uterus-prolapse. Also 2 treatment pages (robotic-sacrocolpopexy, vaginal-prolapse-repair). Review each for completeness and publish when ready. These target exact patient search queries with significant search volume.

title: Fix MedicalBusiness schema — change founder to employee
description: From healthcare SEO audit: MedicalBusiness schema uses 'founder' property linking to Physician, but Dr. Stewart is an employed physician, not the founder. Change to 'employee' or remove the relationship to avoid inaccurate structured data.
close_reason: Changed founder to employee in MedicalBusiness schema

title: Add missing meta descriptions to 5 hub/key pages
description: Five important pages lack custom meta descriptions and fall back to the site-wide default, causing identical SERP snippets across multiple pages. Pages needing unique descriptions: (1) /conditions — conditions.md, (2) /treatments — treatments.md, (3) /blog — blog.md, (4) /conditions/pelvic-organ-prolapse — pelvic-organ-prolapse.md, (5) first blog post 2024-10-14. Add a description field to each page's YAML frontmatter with a compelling, keyword-rich, 150-160 character description.
